description: Repair totals_by_project so it returns numeric totals by project id. Read project ids from project_id, projectId, project, or nested project.id, trim project ids, and skip blank or missing project ids. If an expense_id is present, duplicate expense_ids are revisions: keep only the row with the greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, breaking timestamp ties by later input order, before applying status and totals. Ignore rows whose trimmed and case-normalized status is rejected or void after revision selection. Parse numeric amounts from numbers or numeric strings; missing or blank amount is 0. Preserve first-seen project order based on accepted rows in the returned dict.
